title: The U.S. Is Losing the AI Credibility War—to Itself
summary: Recent restrictions on advanced AI models are undermining critical U.S. cybersecurity outcomes. The administration needs a coherent strategy to work with the private sector, stay ahead of adversaries, keep allies on board, and maintain trust in the U.S. AI stack.

title: China, Not Europe, is the Source of the World’s Imbalances
summary: How Ireland and China’s statistical agency pulled the wool over the eyes of the IMF and OECD.

title: U.S.-Colombia Relations
summary: 1903 – 2025 Over the two centuries since Colombia’s independence, the relationship between Washington and Bogotá has evolved into a close economic and security partnership. But it has at times been strained by U.S. intervention, Cold War geopolitics, and the war on drugs.

title: In Memoriam: Alan Greenspan
summary: Former Chairman of the Federal Reserve and CFR Board Director Alan Greenspan passed away on June 22 at age one hundred.

title: A Super El Niño Looks Likely This Year
summary: The rare climate pattern associated with prolonged periods of intense heat now requires ramped up planning by authorities at all levels to prevent deaths and avoid damage that can be severe. So far, government action has fallen far short.

title: Papa's Got A Brand New Bag
summary: It’s Father’s Day so I’ve got three songs (because the dads out there deserve more than one).

title: Liberal Education In America, The AI Challenge, And The Pope
summary: At a mid-April dinner at a D.C. think tank, I was asked to offer a few words on education and artificial intelligence. I observed that constantly improving AI platforms perform wonders – answering complex questions almost instantly, yielding remarkable efficiencies, and producing breathtaking innovations in industry, science, medicine, military affairs, and more.

title: Warsh's Challenges: Monetary Policy (Full Version)
summary: This is an OpEd at the Washington Post. Their title: “How to protect the economy from the ghosts of 1979.” (Really 1951!) I posted an excerpt a month ago. Here is the full version.

title: Teaching The New Loop
summary: We must all learn to execute the new loop—to combine our human expertise with AI to produce private evals that measure how well AI is meeting our goals, then hill-climb against this measure.

title: State-Level Impacts of Key Medicaid Provisions in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act
summary: The authors developed state-level estimates of budget impact and enrollment changes through 2034 associated with key provisions of the July 2025 budget reconciliation law that affect Medicaid, the health insurance program for low-income individuals.

title: The People’s Liberation Army Navy’s Approach to Maintenance Management
summary: The authors of this report examine how the rapid growth and modernization of China’s navy inform maintenance management and activities. The authors also discuss factors that shape China’s ability to maintain and sustain its forces.

title: Why Do Middle and High Schoolers Miss School?
summary: Using data from two surveys administered in late 2025 to nationally representative samples of students and parents, the authors investigate why secondary school-age students are missing school and to what extent these reasons differ by age.

title: Evaluation of Cohort 3 of Project imPACT
summary: This report presents findings about Project imPACT, a reentry program for adults who had been involved in the justice system that focused on holistic programs and approaches to expand access to behavioral health services and reduce recidivism.

title: Insights and Tools to Improve the Applicant Experience with the Personnel Vetting and Security Clearance Process
summary: The authors examine how applicants experience the U.S. government’s personnel vetting process, identify practical strategies for improving the vetting process experience, and develop tools to help applicants navigate the vetting process.

title: NATO 3.0 and American Security Strategy in Europe
summary: The relationship between the United States and Europe is based in the shared economic, security, and civilizational interests of both sides of the Atlantic. U.S. membership in the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) ensures the security of the North Atlantic, protects U.S. trade, and defends Western civilization from threats by China, Russia, and parts of the Islamic world, notably Iran. Legitimate questions...

title: A New U.S.-Led Architecture for the Middle East
summary: When President Donald Trump recently challenged America’s Arab allies to join the Abraham Accords as a bloc, he declared that such a move would make the Middle East “united, powerful, and economically strong, like perhaps no other area in the world.” This was more than a diplomatic appeal. title: Deterring an Attack on the U.S. Trumps Non-Proliferation?
summary: The nuclear risk reduction community is having a rough go of it lately. There has been much consternation at the expiration of the New START nuclear arms treaty earlier this year. This May, the Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty (NPT) Review Conference ended in “ failure ” after the NPT member states did not come to a consensus statement on the state of the nonproliferation regime. title: A Guide to Heritage Sites
summary: The Executive Order President Trump signed last year on National Parks, Smithsonian museums, and monuments was designed to counter those who attempt to “rewrite our Nation’s history.” Another EO included the Institute of Museum and Library Services, which often supports exhibits at private sites. title: Why Does Alberta Want to Leave Canada and Join the U.S.?
summary: Canada’s Texas is threatening divorce, and it could eventually become the 51st state, with serious impacts on how much Americans pay for gasoline. The idea of energy giant province Alberta leaving Canada is moving from bumper sticker to reality as organizers say they’ve collected over 300,000 signatures for a referendum to leave Canada. That’s almost twice the signatures needed, triggering a vote in October where...

title: CER podcast: Unpacking Europe: The path to EU enlargement
summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement.
